title: poa attenuata var. dahurica
intro: Poa attenuata var. dahurica, also known as Dahurian bentgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. Dahurian bentgrass can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems.
